skill.name: nix-patterns
skill.description: NixOS module patterns. Use when creating or editing NixOS and Home Manager modules, adding packages, or configuring programs.
skill.summary: Provides concise NixOS and Home Manager module patterns for package lists, imports, and configuration files.
skill.author: EdgarPost

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged markdown backticks and Nix code fences as Ruby shell execution, but SKILL.md contains documentation examples only. The weak cryptography finding on the description line is a false positive, and the only real risk factor is normal filesystem path guidance for Nix modules.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution False Positive
low_findings.description: The detected backticks are markdown inline code markers and Nix fenced examples, not Ruby or shell execution. Lines 9 through 12 describe paths, and lines 15 through 35 show declarative Nix snippets without command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain markdown formatting and Nix configuration examples only. No shell interpreter, command invocation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography at the description line, but line 3 only describes NixOS and Home Manager module usage. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence line is plain frontmatter text with no crypto API or algorithm name. This is a clear scanner mismatch.

low_findings.title: Relative Filesystem Path Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill teaches relative Nix module paths and config file destinations. This is expected for the domain, but users should review generated paths before applying changes to a real system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit filesystem paths and an import path. The pattern is legitimate documentation, but it can influence generated edits to user configuration files.

content.user_title: Configure NixOS Modules With Reusable Patterns
content.value_statement: NixOS module work often needs consistent file layout and import patterns. This skill gives concise examples for packages, modules, and Home Manager structure.
actual_capabilities: Explains common directories for Home Manager, NixOS modules, hosts, and home entry points.
Shows how to add packages with conditional platform-specific package selection.
Provides a compact pattern for creating a Home Manager module.
Demonstrates writing application configuration through Home Manager file declarations.
Shows where to import a new module from the home configuration entry point.
limitations: It does not validate Nix syntax or run evaluation commands.
It does not cover flakes, overlays, secrets, or deployment workflows.
It provides patterns, not a complete NixOS architecture guide.
It assumes the user already has a Nix repository structure.

use_cases.title: Add a user package module
use_cases.description: Create a small Home Manager module that adds packages and follows the existing repository layout.
use_cases.target_user: Linux user maintaining a personal Nix configuration

use_cases.title: Organize host configuration
use_cases.description: Place machine-specific settings in the expected host directory and keep shared modules separate.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineer managing multiple NixOS machines

use_cases.title: Review generated Nix changes
use_cases.description: Compare proposed module imports and configuration file declarations against simple reference patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Code reviewer checking Nix configuration updates

prompt_templates.title: Add a Home Manager package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nix-patterns skill to add a Home Manager module for a user package. Explain the file location and import path.

prompt_templates.title: Create a program configuration module
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nix-patterns skill to create a module that installs a package and writes one application configuration file.

prompt_templates.title: Split shared and host settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nix-patterns skill to suggest where shared modules and host-specific settings should live in this Nix repository.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a module import plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nix-patterns skill to review this planned Nix module addition for path consistency, import placement, and platform-specific package handling.

output_examples.input: Add a Home Manager module for a terminal tool.
output_examples.output: The response proposes a module under the Home Manager module directory, lists the package entry, and names the home entry point import.

output_examples.input: Create a module that writes an app configuration file.
output_examples.output: The response describes a Home Manager module that installs the package and manages the application config file declaratively.

output_examples.input: Review where a new module should be imported.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the main home entry point as the import location and checks that the relative path matches the module directory.

best_practices: Keep reusable modules separate from host-specific configuration.
Use conditional package lists for platform-specific dependencies.
Review relative import paths before rebuilding a system.
anti_patterns: Mixing host-specific settings into shared Home Manager modules.
Adding platform-specific packages without a platform condition.
Creating modules without importing them from the expected entry point.

faq.question: Does this skill run Nix commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation patterns and does not execute Nix, shell, or build commands.

faq.question: Can it create complete NixOS configurations?
faq.answer: It can guide small module additions, but it is not a complete configuration framework.

faq.question: Does it support Home Manager?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples focus on Home Manager modules, package lists, and managed config files.

faq.question: Does it cover flakes?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not include flake-specific patterns or commands.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: It is safe as documentation, but generated configuration changes should be reviewed before applying them.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
